executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a massive ransomware wave affecting over 100 organizations in the past 48 hours, including critical sectors like healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital), public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board), and financial services (National Money Mart with database leaked). The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for three critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities that require immediate patching. High-profile Canadian brands Canada Goose and Kids & Company were among victims, alongside municipalities, law firms, and medical facilities. This represents an extraordinary concentration of attacks targeting Canadian infrastructure.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors in 48 hours. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 for critical Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities requiring immediate patching. Critical infrastructure including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal), municipalities (North Perth), and financial services (National Money Mart with database leaked) have been breached.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (breached by both CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross, Kids & Company
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest ransomware), Morison Insurance Brokers

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added eight actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including three critical Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager flaws (CVE-2026-20122, CVE-2026-20128, CVE-2026-20133) being exploited in the wild. North Korea's Lazarus APT stole $290 million from KelpDAO in a crypto heist and deployed new malware targeting C-level credentials. Canada Life suffered a major breach exposing 5.6 million Salesforce records after ShinyHunters compromised employee accounts, with an April 21 extortion deadline. Over 6,000 Apache ActiveMQ instances remain publicly exposed to CVE-2026-34197, and phishing campaigns continue to surge using OAuth abuse, ClickFix techniques, and GitHub issue alerts.

items.targets: KelpDAO ($290M crypto heist), C-level executives for credential theft
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), node compromise and DDoS to enable fraudulent transactions

canada_landscape.summary: Canada Life suffered a significant breach exposing 5.6 million Salesforce records after ShinyHunters compromised employee accounts, with an April 21 extortion deadline. No CCCS advisories were identified in this collection period. Mexican cybersecurity firm BePrime breach exposed Cisco infrastructure used by Canadian clients.
canada_landscape.financial: Canada Life Assurance - 5.6 million Salesforce records exposed following employee account compromise by ShinyHunters threat actor, extortion deadline set for April 21, 2026
